source_docs.title: Prokaryotic single-cell RNA sequencing by in situ combinatorial indexing
Prokaryotic single-cell RNA sequencing by in situ combinatorial indexing

oligos.name: Round 1 RT barcoded random-hexamer primer (BC1)
oligos.role: Barcoded reverse-transcription primer; in-cell first-strand synthesis and first (RT) cell barcode

components.name: Round 1 5' ligation handle (universal)
components.role: constant; accepts Round 2 ligation at 5' phosphate

components.name: Random hexamer (RT priming)
components.role: random primer that anneals to mRNA

oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 3, 96 Round-1 oligos. 5' modification /5Phos/ (required for downstream ligation). Full example: /5Phos/GCCAGACAGAGAANNNNNN. The 7-nt barcodes all begin with C or G. No UMI in Round 1.

oligos.name: Round 2 ligation barcode oligo (BC2)
oligos.role: Second (ligation) cell barcode, added by split-pool T4 ligation

components.name: Round 2 5' handle (universal)
components.role: constant; accepts Round 3 ligation at 5' phosphate

components.name: Round 2 3' handle (universal)
components.role: constant; ligates via linker SB83 to the GCCAGA end of BC1

oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 3, 96 Round-2 oligos. 5' modification /5Phos/. Full example: /5Phos/GCTTCGCGACCTTACCTCCTAC. Annealed to linker SB83 before ligation.

oligos.name: Round 3 ligation barcode + UMI oligo (BC3)
oligos.role: Third (ligation) cell barcode, 7-nt UMI, and Read 1 / P5-side handle

components.name: Partial TruSeq Read 1 handle (PETRI)
components.role: Read 1 primer binding / P5-side PCR handle

components.name: UMI
components.role: 7 degenerate nt, distinguishes transcripts from PCR duplicates

components.name: Round 3 3' handle (universal)
components.role: constant; ligates via linker SB80 to the GCTTCGC end of BC2

oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 3, 96 Round-3 oligos. Full example: AGAATACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCTNNNNNNNGTGTGAAGGTCCTTG. UMI = NNNNNNN (7 nt). Annealed to linker SB80 before ligation. Downstream demux linker GGTCCTTGGCTTCGC.

oligos.name: Round 2 linker oligo (SB83)
oligos.role: Bridging/splint oligo that anneals across the BC2-BC1 junction for Round 2 ligation
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. S = C/G, W = A/T (IUPAC, kept literal). Reverse complement of the CCTCCTACGCCAGA junction. Also used as the 16-base ligation linker in ligation-efficiency tests.

oligos.name: Round 3 linker oligo (SB80)
oligos.role: Bridging/splint oligo that anneals across the BC3-BC2 junction for Round 3 ligation
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. W = A/T. Reverse complement of the GGTCCTTGGCTTCGC junction. Used at 70 uM.

oligos.name: Round 2 blocking oligo 1 (SB84)
oligos.role: Blocking oligo (self-annealed hairpin) that caps unreacted Round 2 linker/barcode ends after ligation
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. S = C/G. Annealed to form an intramolecular hairpin (95->20 C ramp) before use.

oligos.name: Round 2 blocking oligo 2 (SB85)
oligos.role: Blocking oligo that caps unreacted Round 2 ends after ligation
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. W = A/T, S = C/G.

oligos.name: Round 3 blocking oligo 1 (SB81)
oligos.role: Blocking oligo (self-annealed hairpin) that caps unreacted Round 3 linker/barcode ends after ligation
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Annealed to a hairpin before use.

oligos.name: Round 3 blocking oligo 2 (SB82)
oligos.role: Blocking oligo that caps unreacted Round 3 ends after ligation
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. W = A/T.

oligos.name: Template Switch Oligo (TSO, SB14)
oligos.role: Template-switching oligo used in the alternative (template-switch) second-strand route
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Terminal rGrGrG folded to GGG. Used only in the template-switching alternative to second-strand synthesis; standard PETRI-seq uses NEBNext second-strand synthesis instead.

oligos.name: PCR primer after template switch (SB15)
oligos.role: PCR primer (pairs with SB86) for the template-switch route
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Anneals to the TSO handle installed by SB14.

oligos.name: PCR primer after template switch (SB86)
oligos.role: PCR primer (pairs with SB15 or SB13); anneals to the ligated Read-1 handle
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Also used in ligation-efficiency qPCR (amplifies only ligated product).

oligos.name: Hexamer for RT without barcoding (SB94)
oligos.role: Non-barcoded random-hexamer RT primer for bulk control libraries
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Bulk RNA-seq control primer; not used in single-cell barcoding.

oligos.name: rpsB-specific RT primer (SB10)
oligos.role: Gene-specific RT primer used in qPCR validation of in situ RT
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. 5' modification /5Biosg/ (biotin). qPCR/validation only.

oligos.name: rpsB qPCR primer (SB5)
oligos.role: qPCR primer for rpsB cDNA quantification
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Validation only.

oligos.name: rpsB qPCR primer (SB6)
oligos.role: qPCR primer for rpsB cDNA quantification
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Validation only.

oligos.name: SB10-binding rpsB qPCR primer (SB12)
oligos.role: qPCR primer (with SB13) that anneals to the SB10 gene-specific RT primer
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Validation only.

oligos.name: rpsB qPCR primer (SB13)
oligos.role: qPCR primer (with SB12/SB86/SB115)
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Validation only.

oligos.name: rpsB-specific RT primer for ligation test (SB110)
oligos.role: RT primer used in the barcode-ligation efficiency assay
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. 5' /5Phos/. Validation only.

oligos.name: Linker for ligation test (SB111)
oligos.role: 30-base linker control for ligation-efficiency assay
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Validation only.

oligos.name: Ligated primer for ligation test (SB113)
oligos.role: Primer to be ligated in the ligation-efficiency assay
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Validation only.

oligos.name: rpsB-specific RT primer, positive control (SB114)
oligos.role: Positive control mimicking 100%-efficient ligation
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Contains primer sites for both SB86 and SB115. Validation only.

oligos.name: qPCR primer for ligation test (SB115)
oligos.role: qPCR primer (with SB13) that anneals to the RT-primer overhang (all RT product)
oligos.notes: Supplementary Table 2. Validation only.

oligos.name: P5 / i5 library PCR primer (NEBNext i5, E7600)
oligos.role: P5-side indexing PCR primer; installs P5, i5 sample index, and TruSeq Read 1

components.name: TruSeq Read 1
components.role: Read 1 primer binding site; anneals to the PETRI AGAAT...GATCT handle

oligos.notes: Protocol: 'i50x primer (NEB kit E7600S)' or the same from IDT. Anneals to the round-3 AGAATACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT handle.

oligos.name: N70x / i7 library PCR primer (Nextera Index Kit v2)
oligos.role: Read 2 / P7-side indexing PCR primer; installs Nextera Read 2, i7 sample index, and P7

components.name: Mosaic End (ME)
components.role: transposon mosaic end at the tagmented cDNA junction

oligos.notes: Protocol: 2.5 uL N70x primer from Nextera Index Kit v2 Set A (TG-131-2001). Anneals to the Nextera adapter inserted during tagmentation.

oligos.name: EZ-Tn5 transposon adapter (SB117/SB118)
oligos.role: Optional loaded Tn5 mosaic-end adapter (alternative to Nextera XT)

components.name: Tn5 transferred strand (SB118)
components.role: top / transferred strand (Nextera s7 + mosaic end)

oligos.notes: Materials section: anneal SB117 (/5Phos/CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT) with SB118 (GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG), load onto EZ-Tn5. Only used if substituting EZ-Tn5 for the Nextera XT kit.

oligos.name: Illumina TruSeq Read 1 sequencing primer
oligos.role: Sequencing primer for Read 1 (UMI + three cell barcodes)
oligos.notes: Standard Illumina Read 1 primer; reads the UMI and BC3/BC2/BC1. Installed by the i5 PCR primer.

oligos.name: Nextera Read 2 sequencing primer
oligos.role: Sequencing primer for Read 2 (cDNA insert)
oligos.notes: Standard Nextera Read 2 primer; reads the cDNA from the tagmented end.

final_library.source_label: PETRI-seq final paired-end Illumina library (Nextera / TruSeq hybrid)
annotation_lines: CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC = Nextera Read 2 adapter (mosaic end + s7)

evidence.locator: PETRI-seq final paired-end Illumina library (Nextera / TruSeq hybrid)

library_generation.title: In situ reverse transcription (Round 1 barcode)
library_generation.summary: Fixed, permeabilized cells are split across a 96-well plate and reverse-transcribed with well-specific barcoded random hexamers, tagging cDNA with the first cell barcode.
library_generation.note: Maxima H Minus reverse transcriptase extends the random-hexamer RT primer on cellular mRNA inside intact cells; the 5' /5Phos/ handle (GCCAGA + BC1) is carried on the cDNA for later ligation.

library_generation.title: Round 2 split-pool barcode ligation (+ blocking)
library_generation.summary: Cells are pooled, re-split, and a second barcode is ligated onto the 5' handle of the first-strand cDNA using splint linker SB83; unreacted ends are then capped with blocking oligos SB84/SB85.
library_generation.note: T4 DNA ligase joins the 3' handle (CCTCCTAC) of the Round 2 oligo to the 5' phosphate (GCCAGA) of BC1, guided by linker SB83. Blocking prevents further ligation to unbarcoded ends.

library_generation.title: Round 3 split-pool barcode + UMI ligation (+ blocking)
library_generation.summary: Cells are pooled and re-split again; a third oligo carrying the UMI, third barcode, and the Read 1 / P5 handle is ligated via linker SB80. Unreacted ends are capped with blocking oligos SB81/SB82.
library_generation.note: T4 DNA ligase joins the 3' handle (GGTCCTTG) of the Round 3 oligo to the 5' phosphate (GCTTCGC) of BC2, guided by linker SB80. After three rounds each cDNA carries 1 of ~884,736 barcode combinations plus a 7-nt UMI.

library_generation.title: Cell lysis and cDNA purification
library_generation.summary: ~10,000 barcoded cells are lysed with proteinase K and the barcoded single-stranded cDNA is purified with AMPure XP beads.
library_generation.note: Combinatorial barcoding is complete inside cells; lysis releases the labelled cDNA. Every barcoded molecule is non-recoverable if lost, so purification carries the entire library forward.

library_generation.title: Second-strand synthesis
library_generation.summary: NEBNext second-strand synthesis converts the barcoded single-stranded cDNA into double-stranded cDNA.
library_generation.note: Chosen over template switching, which gave lower yield. Produces the double-stranded template required for tagmentation.

library_generation.title: Tagmentation (Nextera XT / EZ-Tn5)
library_generation.summary: Double-stranded cDNA is fragmented and tagged with Nextera mosaic-end adapters; only fragments retaining the P5-side barcode handle and an N7 mosaic end are amplifiable.
library_generation.note: Tn5 inserts the mosaic end (AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG / CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT) into the dsDNA. PCR later uses the transposon-inserted sequence plus the upstream Read 1 handle as primer sites, so only correctly tagmented, barcoded molecules amplify (excluding undigested genomic DNA).

library_generation.title: Indexing PCR (final library)
library_generation.summary: Limited-cycle PCR with the i5 (P5) primer and the Nextera N7 (i7) primer appends P5, both sample indices, and P7 to yield the final paired-end Illumina library.
library_generation.note: i5 primer anneals to the AGAAT...GATCT Read 1 handle and adds P5 + i5 + TruSeq Read 1; the N7/i7 Nextera primer anneals to the mosaic-end adapter and adds Read 2 + i7 + P7. ~16-19 total cycles, monitored by qPCR.

library_sequencing.note: TruSeq Read 1 primer anneals to the bottom strand and extends rightward across the 7-nt UMI, then BC3, linker GGTCCTTGGCTTCGC, BC2, linker CCTCCTACGCCAGA, and BC1 (barcodes read 3→2→1). Barcodes overlap the adjacent linker by one base.
library_sequencing.diagram:
primer:               5'-ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT----------------------------->  read 1 (58 cyc)
top:    5'- ...GAGATCTACAC[i5]ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT NNNNNNN NNNNNNN GGTCCTTGGCTTCGC NNNNNNN CCTCCTACGCCAGA NNNNNNN NNNNNN [cDNA]... -3'
bottom: 3'- ...CTCTAGATGTG[i5]TGTGAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA NNNNNNN NNNNNNN CCAGGAACCGAAGCG NNNNNNN GGAGGATGCGGTCT NNNNNNN NNNNNN [cDNA]... -5'
                                                            UMI     BC3       linker         BC2       linker         BC1    hex

library_sequencing.note: Nextera Read 2 primer (= bottom-strand s7 + mosaic end) anneals to the top-strand adapter and extends leftward into the cDNA from the tagmented junction; reads map adjacent to the Tn5 insertion site (mild bias toward the 5' end of operons).
library_sequencing.diagram:
top:    5'- ...NNNNNN[cDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[i7]... -3'
bottom: 3'- ...NNNNNN[cDNA]GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG[i7]... -5'
primer:  read 2 (17 cyc) <-----------5'-GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG
                    (cDNA insert)          (Nextera R2 / s7 + ME)

library_sequencing.note: 8-cycle i7 sample-index read off the Nextera P7-side adapter; primer anneals to the bottom strand and reads the i7 rightward.
library_sequencing.diagram:
primer: 5'- ...CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC------->  index 1 (8 cyc)
top:    5'- ...CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTCCGAGCCCACGAGAC NNNNNNNN ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
bottom: 3'- ...GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGAGGCTCGGGTGCTCTG NNNNNNNN TAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'
                                              i7            (P7')

library_sequencing.note: 8-cycle i5 sample-index read (Index 2) off the P5-side adapter. On the NextSeq 500/550 (reverse-complement workflow) i5 is read from the regenerated bottom strand, so the recorded index is the reverse complement of the top-strand i5.
library_sequencing.diagram:
top:    5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC NNNNNNNN ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT ... -3'
bottom: 3'- TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTG NNNNNNNN TGTGAGAAAGGGATGTGCTGCGAGAAGGCTAGA ... -5'
primer:                       index 2 <-------NNNNNNNN-3' (i5 read primer, NextSeq bottom strand)
                          (P5)          i5

title: PETRI-seq
description: PETRI-seq (Prokaryotic Expression profiling by Tagging RNA In situ and sequencing) is a high-throughput bacterial single-cell RNA-seq method based on in situ combinatorial indexing. Fixed, permeabilized Gram-negative (E. coli) or Gram-positive (S. aureus) cells are barcoded in place: in-cell reverse transcription with barcoded random hexamers (Round 1) is followed by two rounds of split-pool barcode ligation (Rounds 2 and 3), giving each cell's cDNA a unique three-barcode combination (~884,736 possible) plus a 7-nt UMI. After pooling and lysis, second-strand synthesis and Nextera XT tagmentation build a paired-end Illumina library. Read 1 reads the UMI and the three cell barcodes; Read 2 reads the cDNA insert.

reference.label: Prokaryotic single-cell RNA sequencing by in situ combinatorial indexing

original_publication.title: Prokaryotic single-cell RNA sequencing by in situ combinatorial indexing

authors.name: Sydney B. Blattman
authors.affiliation: Departments of Biological Sciences, Systems Biology, and Biochemistry and Molecular Biophysics, Columbia University, New York City, NY, USA

authors.name: Wenyan Jiang

authors.affiliation: Departments of Biological Sciences, Systems Biology, and Biochemistry and Molecular Biophysics, Columbia University, New York City, NY, USA

authors.name: Saeed Tavazoie
authors.affiliation: Departments of Biological Sciences, Systems Biology, and Biochemistry and Molecular Biophysics, Columbia University, New York City, NY, USA

throughput.summary: Split-pool combinatorial indexing across 96x96x96 wells gives ~884,736 barcode combinations; ~10,000 cells lysed per library (~30,000 E. coli + S. aureus sequenced across the study) at US$0.056 per cell including sequencing.
throughput.cells: Tens of thousands of cells per experiment; ~10,000 cells lysed per library; multiplet frequency ~1.5% at 10,000 cells (Poisson expectation 0.56-0.85%).
throughput.rna: Median >200 mRNA UMIs per exponential-phase E. coli cell (median 227 in optimized experiment 2.01; 27 for stationary phase; 43 for S. aureus); estimated 2.5-10% capture of the ~2,000-8,000 mRNAs per E. coli cell.

publication.statistical_model: Poisson (multiplet-frequency modeling and transcriptional-noise analysis, sigma^2/mu ~ mu; negative binomial regression via sctransform used as an alternative normalization)

other.label: Sequencing
other.value: Illumina NextSeq 500/550, 75 cycles: 58 cyc Read 1 (UMI+barcodes), 17 cyc Read 2 (cDNA), 8 cyc Index 1, 8 cyc Index 2; ~50M reads/library

other.label: Organisms
other.value: Gram-negative E. coli MG1655 and Gram-positive S. aureus USA300

other.label: Notable finding
other.value: Detected rare prophage phi-SA3usa induction in ~0.04% of S. aureus cells

other.label: Related method
other.value: Adapts eukaryotic SPLiT-seq / sci-RNA-seq in situ combinatorial indexing to bacteria; random hexamers replace poly(dT)

data_processing.summary: Reads are quality-trimmed, the 7-nt UMI is extracted from read 1, and read pairs are demultiplexed by their three cell barcodes (BC3→BC2→BC1, each anchored to its downstream linker with ≤1 mismatch). An inclusive knee threshold selects candidate barcode combinations (BCs); read 2 cDNA is aligned to the E. coli/S. aureus genomes with bwa, operons are annotated with featureCounts (RegulonDB/ProOpDB), PCR duplicates are collapsed to UMIs with umi_tools directional grouping, and an operon-by-BC UMI count matrix is built (anti-sense removed, per-cell UMI thresholding) for PCA / sctransform clustering.
data_processing.statistical_model: Poisson (multiplet-frequency and transcriptional-noise modeling, σ²/μ ~ μ); negative binomial via sctransform for alternative normalization
